claim_boundary: Exact-row, same-host, bounded decode-at-depth snapshot for Llama 3.2 3B Instruct Q8_0 on one Apple M4 (16GB): 64 greedy tokens after ~1.5k and ~8k prompts, three same-session alternating rounds, after the split-K kv16 mirror change. Camelid reads PARITY-CLASS with llama.cpp at 8k (median 18.72 vs 18.92, overlapping round bands) and slightly below it at 1.5k (25.22 vs 25.69, llama.cpp above in every round); MLX-LM leads both runtimes at depth (26.51 / 21.84). No Camelid win is claimed on this lane.

workload.model_row: Llama 3.2 3B Instruct Q8_0 (GGUF for camelid and llama.cpp; mlx-community 8-bit for MLX-LM)
workload.depths: ~1500-token and ~8012-token prompts (same text files), 64 greedy tokens
workload.mlx_command: mlx_lm.generate verbose, 64 tokens after the same prompt files

notes: Prior same-host session (bundle ...20260605T022916Z-head-d7c2940) read camelid 16.94 at 8k vs llama.cpp 19.14: the half-width KV mirrors closed the 8k gap to parity-class. Camelid greedy outputs were consistent across rounds and the anchored-recall continuation stayed intact at both depths.
